content:
🚀 office-website | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a private, in-browser document editing solution for common office file formats, eliminating the need for server-side processing.
✨ Key Features: • Multi-Format Support (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx) • Local-First Processing for Enhanced Privacy • Fast and Responsive User Interface (Next.js 15+) • Rich Editing Capabilities • Persistent Local Storage (IndexedDB)
📖 Summary: This repository provides a serverless, client-side Office suite that allows users to edit Word, Excel, and PowerPoint documents directly in their browser using WebAssembly. It prioritizes user privacy by processing all data locally, with no backend interaction required.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 mcp-server | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To serve as a bridge for AI assistants to access and query real-time and historical financial market data through a standardized MCP interface.
✨ Key Features: • Provides access to income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements. • Enables retrieval of current and historical stock prices. • Fetches company news and available crypto tickers. • Supports historical cryptocurrency price data.
📖 Summary: This Python repository provides a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that exposes financial data from Financial Datasets. It allows AI assistants like Claude to access and retrieve various financial statements, stock prices, and company news through a defined interface.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 react-pdf | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Generating PDF documents programmatically using React components for both client-side and server-side applications.
✨ Key Features: • Create PDF files using React components • Render PDFs in the browser (DOM) • Render PDFs on the server (Node.js) • Styling using StyleSheet API • Supports Flexbox layout
📖 Summary: React-pdf/renderer is a React renderer for creating PDF documents on both the browser and server. It allows developers to build PDFs using React components and styling, similar to how they build web UIs.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 opencode | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide developers with an AI-powered coding assistant through a user-friendly interface, streamlining the development process.
✨ Key Features: • Open-source AI coding agent • Command-line interface (CLI) • Desktop application (BETA) • Multiple installation methods (curl, npm, scoop, choco, Homebrew, pacman, AUR, mise, nix) • Customizable installation directory
📖 Summary: OpenCode is an open-source AI coding agent designed to assist developers. It provides a command-line interface and a desktop application for seamless integration into development workflows, offering AI-powered coding assistance.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Load-Monitor | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automated resource management and throttling of runaway processes on Linux systems with cgroups v2 enabled.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time system metrics collection (CPU, memory, load average) • Per-process monitoring • Rule-based resource limiting • CPU and memory restriction via cgroups v2 • Configurable duration thresholds
📖 Summary: Load-Monitor is a Rust-based daemon for Linux that automatically throttles resource-intensive processes using cgroups v2. It monitors CPU and RAM usage, applying configurable rules to limit or terminate processes that exceed defined thresholds.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
